detect_stage.header:
import pytest
from app.ai.stages import detect_stage, VIEWING_DISCUSSION, VIEWING_BOOKED

funcs.test_only_last_8_messages_considered_and:
def test_only_last_8_messages_considered_and_latest_message_priority():
    # 9 messages, only last 8 considered, latest triggers booking
    messages = [{"message": "irrelevant"}] * 1 + [{"message": "booking confirmed"}] * 8
    # Assuming "booking confirmed" matches BOOKED_PATTERNS (simulate by using a known booking keyword)
    # Since exact BOOKED_PATTERNS unknown, we simulate by assuming "booking" triggers VIEWING_BOOKED
    # So latest message triggers VIEWING_BOOKED
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_BOOKED

    # If latest message triggers discussion, should return VIEWING_DISCUSSION
    messages = [{"message": "irrelevant"}] * 1 + [{"message": "rearrange this"}] * 8
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION
funcs.test_discussion_patterns_in_recent_messa:
def test_discussion_patterns_in_recent_messages_triggers_viewing_discussion():
    # Recent messages (up to last 4) contain DISCUSSION_PATTERNS combined with negation or "or"/"instead"
    # We simulate by putting "or" and "rearrange" in last 4 messages
    messages = [
        {"message": "Booking confirmed"},
        {"message": "We can rearrange or reschedule"},
        {"message": "Another time maybe"},
        {"message": "instead of today"},
    ]
    # Should detect VIEWING_DISCUSSION because of combined patterns in recent messages
    assert detect_stage(messages) == VIEWING_DISCUSSION

funcs.test_considers_only_last_8_messages_and_:
def test_considers_only_last_8_messages_and_processes_chronologically():
    # 10 messages, only last 8 considered
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 12, 0)
    msgs = []
    # First two messages with valid time but should be ignored (too old)
    msgs.append(make_message("Booked for 9am"))
    msgs.append(make_message("Discussion at 10am"))
    # Next 8 messages, only last 3 have no booked/discussion but have time, so candidates
    # Messages 3-5 have booked pattern and time
    msgs.append(make_message("Booked for 11am"))
    msgs.append(make_message("Discussion at 12pm"))
    msgs.append(make_message("Booked at 1pm"))
    # Messages 6-10 have time but no booked/discussion, only last 3 of these 8 considered candidates anyway
    msgs.append(make_message("See you at 2pm"))
    msgs.append(make_message("Meeting at 3pm"))
    msgs.append(make_message("Call at 4pm"))
    msgs.append(make_message("Chat at 5pm"))
    msgs.append(make_message("Talk at 6pm"))

    # last 8 are msgs[2:] (index 0-based)
    # last 3 of these last 8 are msgs[7], msgs[8], msgs[9] (4pm, 5pm, 6pm)
    # candidates are messages with booked/discussion or last 3 messages with time
    # So candidates are msgs[2], msgs[3], msgs[4], msgs[7], msgs[8], msgs[9]
    # The last candidate is msgs[9] with "Talk at 6pm"

    dt = extract_viewing_datetime(msgs, now=now)
    # Expected time: 6pm = 18:00 on now.date()
    expected = datetime(now.year, now.month, now.day, 18, 0)
    # If candidate datetime < now, add 1 day, but 18:00 > 12:00 so same day
    assert dt == expected
funcs.test_fallback_to_content_key_and_str_con:
def test_fallback_to_content_key_and_str_conversion():
    now = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 9, 0)
    # message key missing or falsy, content used
    msgs = [
        {"content": "Booked for 10am"},
        {"message": None, "content": 12345},  # numeric content, str() -> "12345"
        {"message": ""},  # falsy message, no content key
        {"content": "Discussion at 11am"},
    ]
    # Only last 8 messages, all here
    dt = extract_viewing_datetime(msgs, now=now)
    # Candidates: messages with booked/discussion or last 3 messages with time
    # "Booked for 10am" and "Discussion at 11am" qualify
    # Last candidate is "Discussion at 11am"
    expected = datetime(now.year, now.month, now.day, 11, 0)
    assert dt == expected
funcs.test_target_date_keywords_and_roll_forwa:
def test_target_date_keywords_and_roll_forward_if_past():
    base_now = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 10, 0)  # Saturday

    # "today" keyword, time after now → same day
    msgs = [make_message("Booked for today at 11am")]
    dt = extract_viewing_datetime(msgs, now=base_now)
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 11, 0)
    assert dt == expected

    # "today" keyword, time before now → rolls forward 1 day
    msgs = [make_message("Booked for today at 9am")]
    dt = extract_viewing_datetime(msgs, now=base_now)
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 2, 9, 0)
    assert dt == expected

    # "tomorrow" keyword
    msgs = [make_message("Discussion tomorrow at 10am")]
    dt = extract_viewing_datetime(msgs, now=base_now)
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 2, 10, 0)
    assert dt == expected

    # "day after tomorrow" keyword
    msgs = [make_message("Meeting day after tomorrow at 10am")]
    dt = extract_viewing_datetime(msgs, now=base_now)
    expected = datetime(2024, 6, 3, 10, 0)
    assert dt == expected
funcs.test_weekday_name_and_numeric_date_patte:
def test_weekday_name_and_numeric_date_patterns():
    base_now = datetime(2024, 6, 1, 10, 0)  # Saturday

    # Weekday name "Monday" → next Monday after base_now (June 3, 2024)
    msgs = [make_message("Booked for Monday at 9am")]
    dt = extract_viewing_datetime(msgs, now=base_now)
    expected_date = datetime(2024, 6, 3, 9, 0)
    assert dt == expected_date

    # Numeric date pattern (assume 5/6) without year, date in future this year
    msgs = [make_message("Booked for 5/6 at 10am")]
    dt = extract_viewing_datetime(msgs, now=base_now)
    expected_date = datetime(2024, 6, 5, 10, 0)
    assert dt == expected_date

    # Numeric date pattern (assume 1/1) without year, date passed this year → next year
    msgs = [make_message("Booked for 1/1 at 10am")]
    dt = extract_viewing_datetime(msgs, now=base_now)
    expected_date = datetime(2025, 1, 1, 10, 0)
    assert dt == expected_date

landlord_messages.header:
import pytest
from app.ai.conversation_memory import landlord_messages

funcs.test_exact_matching_sender_and_direction:
def test_exact_matching_sender_and_direction():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord"},
        {"sender": "tenant"},
        {"direction": "INBOUND"},
        {"direction": "outbound"},
        {"sender": "Landlord"},
        {"direction": "inbound"},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"sender": "landlord"},
        {"direction": "INBOUND"},
        {"sender": "Landlord"},
        {"direction": "inbound"},
    ]
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected
funcs.test_fallback_to_direction_when_sender_m:
def test_fallback_to_direction_when_sender_missing_or_none():
    messages = [
        {"direction": "inbound"},
        {"sender": None, "direction": "landlord"},
        {"sender": None, "direction": None},
        {"sender": None},
        {},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"direction": "inbound"},
        {"sender": None, "direction": "landlord"},
    ]
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected
funcs.test_case_insensitivity_and_exact_match:
def test_case_insensitivity_and_exact_match():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "LANDLORD"},
        {"sender": "InBound"},
        {"sender": "landlord "},  # trailing space, should NOT match exactly
        {"direction": " inbound"},  # leading space, should NOT match exactly
        {"sender": "landlord tenant"},  # substring, should NOT match
        {"direction": "inbounded"},  # substring, should NOT match
    ]
    expected = [
        {"sender": "LANDLORD"},
        {"sender": "InBound"},
    ]
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected
funcs.test_order_preservation_and_partial_matc:
def test_order_preservation_and_partial_matches():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "tenant"},
        {"sender": "landlord"},
        {"direction": "inbound"},
        {"sender": "tenant"},
        {"direction": "outbound"},
        {"sender": "landlord"},
    ]
    expected = [
        {"sender": "landlord"},
        {"direction": "inbound"},
        {"sender": "landlord"},
    ]
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == expected
funcs.test_empty_string_and_none_values:
def test_empty_string_and_none_values():
    messages = [
        {"sender": ""},
        {"direction": ""},
        {"sender": None, "direction": ""},
        {"sender": "", "direction": None},
        {"sender": None, "direction": None},
    ]
    # None or empty string fallback to empty string, which does not match "landlord" or "inbound"
    assert landlord_messages(messages) == []

funcs.test_no_landlord_messages_returns_previo:
def test_no_landlord_messages_returns_previous_if_valid():
    # previous is valid LANDLORD_ATTITUDES string (e.g. "aggressive") returned if no landlord messages
    # Spec says only "responsive" known, but example shows "aggressive" works
    # We test with "aggressive" as valid previous
    assert detect_landlord_attitude(None, previous="aggressive") == "aggressive"
    assert detect_landlord_attitude([], previous="aggressive") == "aggressive"
funcs.test_slow_reply_when_two_messages_older_:
def test_slow_reply_when_two_messages_older_than_24h_and_no_patterns():
    # two landlord messages >24h apart, no patterns matched → returns "slow_reply"
    now = datetime.utcnow()
    msgs = [
        landlord_message("neutral message", created_at=now - timedelta(days=2)),
        landlord_message("neutral message", created_at=now),
    ]
    result = detect_landlord_attitude(msgs)
    assert result == "slow_reply"
funcs.test_responsive_when_no_patterns_and_no_:
def test_responsive_when_no_patterns_and_no_slow_reply_condition():
    # landlord messages but no patterns matched and no slow reply condition → returns "responsive"
    now = datetime.utcnow()
    msgs = [
        landlord_message("neutral message", created_at=now),
        landlord_message("neutral message", created_at=now),
    ]
    result = detect_landlord_attitude(msgs)
    assert result == "responsive"
funcs.test_only_last_4_landlord_messages_consi:
def test_only_last_4_landlord_messages_considered_for_pattern_matching():
    # Only last 4 landlord messages considered for pattern matching
    # The 5th last message contains aggressive pattern but last 4 do not → returns not aggressive
    msgs = [
        landlord_message("aggressive pattern here"),  # 5th last
        landlord_message("neutral"),
        landlord_message("neutral"),
        landlord_message("neutral"),
        landlord_message("neutral"),
    ]
    result = detect_landlord_attitude(msgs)
    assert result != "aggressive"

latest_landlord_asked_for_phone.header:
import pytest
from app.ai.conversation_memory import latest_landlord_asked_for_phone

funcs.test_no_landlord_messages_returns_false:
def test_no_landlord_messages_returns_false():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "tenant", "message": "Can I call you?"},
        {"sender": "agent", "message": "Please respond."},
        {"sender": "inboundx", "message": "send your phone"}  # sender not exact match
    ]
    assert latest_landlord_asked_for_phone(messages) is False
funcs.test_message_text_fallback_and_empty_tex:
def test_message_text_fallback_and_empty_text_returns_false():
    # message key missing, fallback to content
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "content": "Please provide your contact"}]
    assert latest_landlord_asked_for_phone(messages) is True

    # both message and content missing -> empty string -> False
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord"}]
    assert latest_landlord_asked_for_phone(messages) is False

    # message and content empty or None -> False
    messages = [
        {"sender": "landlord", "message": ""},
        {"sender": "landlord", "content": None}
    ]
    # last landlord message is content None -> empty string -> False
    assert latest_landlord_asked_for_phone(messages) is False
funcs.test_whatsapp_and_whats_app_variants:
def test_whatsapp_and_whats_app_variants():
    # "whatsapp" single word
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Please share your WhatsApp contact"}]
    assert latest_landlord_asked_for_phone(messages) is True

    # "whats app" with space
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Can you provide your whats app number?"}]
    assert latest_landlord_asked_for_phone(messages) is True

    # "what's" with apostrophe
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Could you give me what's your phone number?"}]
    assert latest_landlord_asked_for_phone(messages) is True

    # "whats" without apostrophe
    messages = [{"sender": "landlord", "message": "Can you send whats your mobile?"}]
    assert latest_landlord_asked_for_phone(messages) is True

landlord_asked_for_phone.header:
import pytest
from app.ai.personas import landlord_asked_for_phone

funcs.test_case_insensitivity_and_word_boundar:
def test_case_insensitivity_and_word_boundaries():
    # Case insensitive matching
    assert landlord_asked_for_phone("Can you SEND me YOUR PHONE number?") is True
    # Keywords embedded in other words do not count
    assert landlord_asked_for_phone("I have a smartphone.") is False
    assert landlord_asked_for_phone("Please shareable info.") is False
    # Punctuation adjacent to keywords allowed
    assert landlord_asked_for_phone("Can you send me your phone, please?") is True
    assert landlord_asked_for_phone("Give your number!") is True
funcs.test_whatsapp_and_whats_app_variants:
def test_whatsapp_and_whats_app_variants():
    # "whatsapp" as a single word
    assert landlord_asked_for_phone("Please share your whatsapp contact.") is True
    # "whats app" with space
    assert landlord_asked_for_phone("Can you give me your whats app number?") is True
    # "whatsapp" as one word (should match "whatsapp")
    assert landlord_asked_for_phone("Send your whatsApp info.") is True
    # "what's" and "whats" with optional apostrophe in second vocab
    assert landlord_asked_for_phone("What's your phone number?") is True
    assert landlord_asked_for_phone("Whats your phone number?") is True

viewing_requested.header:
import pytest
from app.ai.conversation_memory import viewing_requested

funcs.test_message_key_precedence_and_falsy_va:
def test_message_key_precedence_and_falsy_values():
    # message key present and truthy, content ignored
    messages = [{"message": "Is it available?", "content": "No"}]
    assert viewing_requested(messages) is True

    # message key falsy, fallback to content key
    messages = [{"message": "", "content": "Can I come round tomorrow?"}]
    assert viewing_requested(messages) is True

    # both keys falsy or missing, contributes empty string
    messages = [{"message": None, "content": None}, {}]
    assert viewing_requested(messages) is False
funcs.test_case_insensitivity_and_whole_word_m:
def test_case_insensitivity_and_whole_word_matching():
    messages = [
        {"message": "I want to VIEW the property."},
        {"content": "Let's schedule an Appointment."},
        {"message": "Looking forward to seeing it."},
        {"message": "Is it available?"}
    ]
    assert viewing_requested(messages) is True

    # substrings inside longer words do not match
    messages = [{"message": "The viewer is ready."}]
    assert viewing_requested(messages) is False
funcs.test_phrases_with_spaces_and_word_bounda:
def test_phrases_with_spaces_and_word_boundaries():
    # phrases must match exactly with word boundaries
    messages = [{"message": "Can I come round tomorrow?"}]
    assert viewing_requested(messages) is True

    messages = [{"message": "Can I come over later?"}]
    assert viewing_requested(messages) is True

    messages = [{"message": "I want to see it today."}]
    assert viewing_requested(messages) is True

    # partial phrase does not match
    messages = [{"message": "I want to see itis."}]
    assert viewing_requested(messages) is False
funcs.test_non_string_values_converted_to_stri:
def test_non_string_values_converted_to_string():
    messages = [
        {"message": 123},  # str(123) = "123", no keyword
        {"content": True},  # str(True) = "True", no keyword
        {"message": ["come", "round"]},  # str(list) = "['come', 'round']", no keyword as phrase
        {"message": None},  # str(None) = "None"
        {"content": "Available now"}  # keyword present
    ]
    assert viewing_requested(messages) is True

build_prompt.header:
import pytest
from app.ai.replies import build_prompt

outbound_count.header:
import pytest
from app.ai.conversation_memory import outbound_count

funcs.test_outbound_count_none_and_empty:
def test_outbound_count_none_and_empty():
    # None input returns 0
    assert outbound_count(None) == 0
    # Empty list returns 0
    assert outbound_count([]) == 0
funcs.test_outbound_count_fallback_to_directio:
def test_outbound_count_fallback_to_direction_and_case_insensitivity():
    messages = [
        {"sender": None, "direction": "tenant"},
        {"sender": "", "direction": "Inbound"},
        {"sender": False, "direction": "AI"},
        {"sender": "OutBound"},
        {"direction": "user"},
        {"sender": "unknown"}
    ]
    # Matches: tenant, AI, OutBound, user -> 4 total
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 4
funcs.test_outbound_count_missing_keys_and_fal:
def test_outbound_count_missing_keys_and_falsy_values():
    messages = [
        {},  # no keys
        {"sender": None},  # sender falsy, no direction
        {"direction": ""},  # direction empty string
        {"sender": False, "direction": None},  # both falsy
        {"sender": 0, "direction": "user"},  # sender falsy (0), direction "user" matches
    ]
    # Only last message counts (direction "user")
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 1
funcs.test_outbound_count_exact_match_only:
def test_outbound_count_exact_match_only():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "user123"},
        {"direction": "outbound-message"},
        {"sender": "ai assistant"},
        {"direction": "tenant"},
        {"sender": "AI"},
    ]
    # Only exact matches: "tenant" and "AI" count -> 2
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 2
funcs.test_outbound_count_large_varied_input:
def test_outbound_count_large_varied_input():
    messages = [
        {"sender": "User"},
        {"sender": "tenant"},
        {"direction": "OUTBOUND"},
        {"direction": "inbound"},
        {"sender": "AI"},
        {"sender": ""},
        {"direction": None},
        {"sender": "system"},
        {"direction": "user"},
        {"sender": None, "direction": "tenant"},
    ]
    # Matches: User, tenant, OUTBOUND, AI, user, tenant -> 6 total
    assert outbound_count(messages) == 6

tenant_shared_phone.header:
import pytest
from app.ai.personas import tenant_shared_phone

funcs.test_returns_false_for_falsy_mobile_numb:
def test_returns_false_for_falsy_mobile_number():
    # mobile_number is None
    assert tenant_shared_phone([], None) is False
    # mobile_number is empty string
    assert tenant_shared_phone([], "") is False
    # mobile_number is falsy but messages present
    messages = [{"sender": "user", "message": "Call me at 12345"}]
    assert tenant_shared_phone(messages, "") is False
funcs.test_normalization_and_local_number_deri:
def test_normalization_and_local_number_derivation():
    # mobile_number with non-digit chars and UK prefix '44'
    messages = [
        {"sender": "user", "message": "My number is 07123456789"},
        {"sender": "tenant", "content": "Reach me at 447123456789"},
        {"sender": "ai", "message": "Contact 07123456789 now"},
    ]
    # mobile_number with +44 prefix and spaces
    mobile_number = "+44 7123 456789"
    # compact_number = "447123456789"
    # local_number = "07123456789"
    # Both numbers appear in messages content digits, so True expected
    assert tenant_shared_phone(messages, mobile_number) is True
funcs.test_sender_case_insensitivity_and_allow:
def test_sender_case_insensitivity_and_allowed_senders_only():
    mobile_number = "123456"
    messages = [
        {"sender": "USER", "message": "Call 123456 now"},       # allowed sender uppercase
        {"sender": "Tenant", "content": "Number: 123456"},      # allowed sender mixed case
        {"direction": "Outbound", "message": "Dial 123456"},    # allowed sender from direction
        {"sender": "AI", "message": "Number 123456"},           # allowed sender uppercase
        {"sender": "agent", "message": "123456"},               # disallowed sender, ignored
        {"direction": "incoming", "message": "123456"},         # disallowed direction, ignored
        {"message": "123456"},                                   # no sender or direction, ignored
    ]
    assert tenant_shared_phone(messages, mobile_number) is True
funcs.test_message_content_fallback_and_digit_:
def test_message_content_fallback_and_digit_extraction():
    mobile_number = "5551234"
    messages = [
        {"sender": "user", "message": None, "content": "Call me at 555-1234!"},  # message None, content used
        {"sender": "tenant", "message": "", "content": "My number is (555)1234"}, # empty message, content used
        {"sender": "ai", "message": "Reach 5551234 now"},                        # message used
        {"sender": "outbound", "content": None},                                # content None, treated as empty
        {"sender": "user"},                                                     # no message or content keys
    ]
    assert tenant_shared_phone(messages, mobile_number) is True
funcs.test_no_matching_number_returns_false:
def test_no_matching_number_returns_false():
    mobile_number = "999888777"
    messages = [
        {"sender": "user", "message": "Call me at 1234567890"},
        {"sender": "tenant", "content": "My number is 5551234"},
        {"sender": "ai", "message": "Reach me"},
        {"sender": "outbound", "content": "No number here"},
    ]
    assert tenant_shared_phone(messages, mobile_number) is False
funcs.test_empty_or_none_messages_treated_as_n:
def test_empty_or_none_messages_treated_as_no_messages():
    mobile_number = "1234"
    # messages is None
    assert tenant_shared_phone(None, mobile_number) is False
    # messages is empty list
    assert tenant_shared_phone([], mobile_number) is False

funcs.test_returns_true_if_conversation_phone_:
def test_returns_true_if_conversation_phone_number_shared_at_truthy():
    conv = DummyConversation(phone_number_shared_at=True)
    # Should return True immediately regardless of messages or persona
    assert phone_shared_state([], None, conv) is True
    assert phone_shared_state(None, {"mobile_number": "07123456789"}, conv) is True
    assert phone_shared_state([{"sender": "user", "message": "07123456789"}], None, conv) is True
funcs.test_returns_false_if_no_conversation_an:
def test_returns_false_if_no_conversation_and_no_mobile_number():
    # persona is None
    assert phone_shared_state([], None, None) is False
    # persona missing mobile_number
    assert phone_shared_state([], {}, None) is False
    # persona mobile_number is None or empty string
    assert phone_shared_state([], {"mobile_number": None}, None) is False
    assert phone_shared_state([], {"mobile_number": ""}, None) is False
funcs.test_handles_none_or_empty_messages_and_:
def test_handles_none_or_empty_messages_and_missing_keys_gracefully():
    persona = {"mobile_number": "07123456789"}
    # messages is None
    assert phone_shared_state(None, persona, None) is False
    # messages with missing sender and content keys
    messages = [
        {},  # no keys at all
        {"sender": None, "message": None},
        {"direction": None, "content": None},
    ]
    assert phone_shared_state(messages, persona, None) is False

normalize_conversation_style.header:
import pytest
from app.ai.personas import normalize_conversation_style

# Since CONVERSATION_STYLES and STYLE_ALIASES are unspecified,
# we use representative example strings consistent with the spec.

# We assume these example canonical styles and aliases for testing:
# (These are only for test purposes; actual values are unknown.)
CANONICAL_STYLE = "formal_discussion"
ALIAS_STYLE = "casual_chat"
ALIAS_KEY = "casual"

funcs.test_case_sensitivity_no_match:
def test_case_sensitivity_no_match():
    # Case variations do not match (no case normalization)
    # Assuming CANONICAL_STYLE is lowercase, uppercase input returns default
    assert normalize_conversation_style(CANONICAL_STYLE.upper()) == "friendly_viewing"
    # Alias key uppercase also returns default
    assert normalize_conversation_style(ALIAS_KEY.upper()) == "friendly_viewing"

create_conversation.header:
import pytest
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock
from app.db.repository import create_conversation
from app.db.status import NEW_LEAD

# We assume CONVERSATION_STYLES and STYLE_ALIASES are imported or accessible in the module where create_conversation is defined.
# Since we do not have their actual contents, we will patch them in tests that require them.

@pytest.fixture
def mock_session_scope():
    with patch("app.db.repository.session_scope") as mock_scope:
        mock_cm = MagicMock()
        mock_scope.return_value.__enter__.return_value = mock_cm
        yield mock_cm

@pytest.fixture
def mock_conversation_class():
    with patch("app.db.repository.Conversation") as mock_conv:
        instance = MagicMock()
        mock_conv.return_value = instance
        yield mock_conv, instance

funcs.test_database_session_commit_and_refresh:
def test_database_session_commit_and_refresh_called(mock_session_scope, mock_conversation_class):
    mock_conv_class, mock_instance = mock_conversation_class

    thread_id = "threadDB"
    landlord_attitude = "attentive"
    conversation_style = "Formal"
    listing_id = "listingDB"

    from app.db import repository
    repository.CONVERSATION_STYLES = {"Formal"}
    repository.STYLE_ALIASES = {}

    result = create_conversation(thread_id, listing_id, conversation_style, landlord_attitude)

    # session_scope context manager's __enter__ returns mock_session_scope
    # We expect commit and refresh called on the session with the instance
    mock_session_scope.commit.assert_called_once()
    mock_session_scope.refresh.assert_called_once_with(mock_instance)

    assert result is mock_instance

ensure_account_persona.header:
import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
from app.db.repository import ensure_account_persona

class DummyAccount:
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        for k, v in kwargs.items():
            setattr(self, k, v)

@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def patch_db_and_helpers():
    with patch("app.db.repository.get_persona_template") as mock_get_template, \
         patch("app.db.repository.select_persona") as mock_select_persona, \
         patch("app.db.repository.materialize_persona") as mock_materialize, \
         patch("app.db.repository.normalize_conversation_style") as mock_normalize_style, \
         patch("app.db.repository.get_conversation_style") as mock_get_style, \
         patch("app.db.repository.db_session") as mock_db_session, \
         patch("app.db.repository.Account") as mock_account_model:

        yield {
            "get_persona_template": mock_get_template,
            "select_persona": mock_select_persona,
            "materialize_persona": mock_materialize,
            "normalize_conversation_style": mock_normalize_style,
            "get_conversation_style": mock_get_style,
            "db_session": mock_db_session,
            "Account": mock_account_model,
        }

def test_returns_none_if_account_not_found(patch_db_and_helpers):
    # Setup: account_id lookup returns None
    patch_db_and_helpers["Account"].query.filter_by.return_value.first.return_value = None

    result = ensure_account_persona(123)
    assert result is None

def test_fallback_to_select_persona_when_no_template_and_materialize_called(patch_db_and_helpers):
    # Setup account with persona_type set but get_persona_template returns None
    account = DummyAccount(
        id=1,
        email="a@b.com",
        persona_type="nonexistent",
        persona_name=None,
        persona_job=None,
        home_city=None,
        phone_fetching_type=None,
        message_strategy=None,
        escalation_behavior=None,
        conversation_goal=None,
        conversation_style=None,
        persona_partner_name=None,
        persona_partner_job=None,
    )
    patch_db_and_helpers["Account"].query.filter_by.return_value.first.return_value = account
    patch_db_and_helpers["get_persona_template"].return_value = None
    # select_persona returns a dummy template
    dummy_template = {
        "names": {"partner": []},
        "conversation_styles": ["friendly_viewing"],
        "phone_fetching_type": "template_phone",
        "message_strategy": "template_strategy",
        "escalation_behavior": "template_escalation",
        "conversation_goal": "template_goal",
        "household_description": "desc",
        "message_tone": "tone",
        "display_name": "display",
    }
    patch_db_and_helpers["select_persona"].return_value = dummy_template
    patch_db_and_helpers["normalize_conversation_style"].side_effect = lambda s: s.strip()
    patch_db_and_helpers["get_conversation_style"].return_value = {
        "phone_fetching_type": "style_phone",
        "strategy": "style_strategy",
        "escalation_behavior": "style_escalation",
        "conversation_goal": "style_goal",
    }

    result = ensure_account_persona(account)

    # materialize_persona called with selected template and deterministic seed
    patch_db_and_helpers["materialize_persona"].assert_called_once_with(
        dummy_template, f"{account.id}:{account.email}"
    )
    # The returned dict uses account fields and falls back to template and style config
    assert result["persona_type"] == account.persona_type
    assert result["persona_name"] is None
    assert result["phone_fetching_type"] == "template_phone"
    assert result["message_strategy"] == "template_strategy"
    assert result["escalation_behavior"] == "template_escalation"
    assert result["conversation_goal"] == "template_goal"
    assert result["conversation_style"] == "friendly_viewing"
    assert result["conversation_styles"] == ["friendly_viewing"]
    assert result["household_description"] == "desc"
    assert result["message_tone"] == "tone"
    assert result["display_name"] == "display"

def test_missing_partner_fields_trigger_materialize_and_require_partner_fields(patch_db_and_helpers):
    # Template has non-empty partner list, so partner fields required
    template_with_partner = {
        "names": {"partner": ["someone"]},
        "conversation_styles": ["style1"],
        "phone_fetching_type": "tmpl_phone",
        "message_strategy": "tmpl_strategy",
        "escalation_behavior": "tmpl_escalation",
        "conversation_goal": "tmpl_goal",
        "household_description": None,
        "message_tone": None,
        "display_name": None,
    }
    # Account missing persona_partner_name and persona_partner_job
    account = DummyAccount(
        id=2,
        email="x@y.com",
        persona_type="typeA",
        persona_name="Name",
        persona_job="Job",
        home_city="City",
        phone_fetching_type="phone_type",
        message_strategy="msg_strategy",
        escalation_behavior="escalation",
        conversation_goal="goal",
        conversation_style="style1",
        persona_partner_name=None,
        persona_partner_job="PartnerJob",
    )
    patch_db_and_helpers["Account"].query.filter_by.return_value.first.return_value = account
    patch_db_and_helpers["get_persona_template"].return_value = template_with_partner
    patch_db_and_helpers["select_persona"].return_value = template_with_partner
    patch_db_and_helpers["normalize_conversation_style"].side_effect = lambda s: s
    patch_db_and_helpers["get_conversation_style"].return_value = {
        "phone_fetching_type": "style_phone",
        "strategy": "style_strategy",
        "escalation_behavior": "style_escalation",
        "conversation_goal": "style_goal",
    }

    result = ensure_account_persona(account)

    # materialize_persona called because partner_name missing
    patch_db_and_helpers["materialize_persona"].assert_called_once()
    # Returned dict includes partner fields (partner_name updated)
    assert result["persona_partner_name"] is not None
    assert result["persona_partner_job"] == "PartnerJob"

def test_conversation_style_normalization_and_aliases(patch_db_and_helpers):
    # Account missing conversation_style, template provides multiple styles
    template = {
        "names": {"partner": []},
        "conversation_styles": [" casual ", "friendly_viewing"],
        "phone_fetching_type": "tmpl_phone",
        "message_strategy": "tmpl_strategy",
        "escalation_behavior": "tmpl_escalation",
        "conversation_goal": "tmpl_goal",
        "household_description": None,
        "message_tone": None,
        "display_name": None,
    }
    account = DummyAccount(
        id=3,
        email="z@z.com",
        persona_type="typeB",
        persona_name="NameB",
        persona_job="JobB",
        home_city="CityB",
        phone_fetching_type=None,
        message_strategy=None,
        escalation_behavior=None,
        conversation_goal=None,
        conversation_style=None,  # missing, triggers default from template
        persona_partner_name=None,
        persona_partner_job=None,
    )
    patch_db_and_helpers["Account"].query.filter_by.return_value.first.return_value = account
    patch_db_and_helpers["get_persona_template"].return_value = template
    patch_db_and_helpers["select_persona"].return_value = template

    # normalize_conversation_style trims and maps aliases (simulate alias mapping)
    def normalize_style(s):
        s = s.strip()
        aliases = {"casual": "friendly_viewing", "friendly": "friendly_viewing"}
        return aliases.get(s, s)
    patch_db_and_helpers["normalize_conversation_style"].side_effect = normalize_style

    patch_db_and_helpers["get_conversation_style"].return_value = {
        "phone_fetching_type": "style_phone",
        "strategy": "style_strategy",
        "escalation_behavior": "style_escalation",
        "conversation_goal": "style_goal",
    }

    result = ensure_account_persona(account)

    # conversation_style normalized from first template style " casual " -> "friendly_viewing"
    assert result["conversation_style"] == "friendly_viewing"
    # conversation_styles list preserved from template
    assert result["conversation_styles"] == [" casual ", "friendly_viewing"]

def test_priority_order_for_phone_fetching_type_message_strategy_escalation_behavior(patch_db_and_helpers):
    # Account missing escalation_behavior, message_strategy; template missing phone_fetching_type
    template = {
        "names": {"partner": []},
        "conversation_styles": ["styleX"],
        "phone_fetching_type": None,
        "message_strategy": "tmpl_msg_strategy",
        "escalation_behavior": None,
        "conversation_goal": "tmpl_goal",
        "household_description": None,
        "message_tone": None,
        "display_name": None,
    }
    account = DummyAccount(
        id=4,
        email="p@q.com",
        persona_type="typeC",
        persona_name="NameC",
        persona_job="JobC",
        home_city="CityC",
        phone_fetching_type="acct_phone_type",
        message_strategy=None,
        escalation_behavior=None,
        conversation_goal=None,
        conversation_style="styleX",
        persona_partner_name=None,
        persona_partner_job=None,
    )
    patch_db_and_helpers["Account

generate_message_persona_prompt.header:
import pytest
from app.ai.prompts import generate_message_persona_prompt

funcs.test_persona_conversation_goal_and_messa:
def test_persona_conversation_goal_and_message_tone_fallbacks():
    # persona with conversation_goal and missing message_tone (should fallback to "brief, casual, realistic")
    persona = {
        "persona_name": "Alice",
        "conversation_goal": "Secure a viewing appointment",
    }
    prompt = generate_message_persona_prompt(
        conversation="Hi, I am interested in the flat.",
        stage="AI_REPLIED",
        persona=persona,
        conversation_style=None,
    )
    # conversation_goal from persona should appear
    assert "Secure a viewing appointment" in prompt
    # message_tone fallback to "brief, casual, realistic"
    assert "brief, casual, realistic" in prompt
